customer_anxiety_profile: Mildred is 84, on a walker, has a bad back, uses a hearing aid (batteries were dead during this call), and has a helper who comes one day a week. She is warm and cooperative but physically fatigued and managing multiple concurrent home projects. Her primary concern is cost transparency — she asked twice about what she will be charged and whether costs will 'keep going up.' She is not hostile to solar; she is cautious about adding another bill.
unresolved_concerns: Cost structure: 'You tell me how much money that it's gonna cost me' and 'does it keep going up, up, and up?' — Mildred asked this directly and received only a deferral to the appointment.
Bill not collected: Parker scheduled a Friday morning callback to get the bill via Mildred's granddaughter. If that callback fails, the closer arrives without a design.
Title/co-owners: Never asked. Unknown whether a family member is on the title or functions as a co-decider.
sensitive_topics_raised: Physical health: exhaustion, bad back, walker-dependent, cannot drive
Hearing impairment: hearing aids with dead batteries during the call
Age and independence: 84 years old, relies on a weekly helper and family for transportation and tasks
Financial caution: explicit concern about costs increasing over time, framed against existing utility bill pain
exact_promises_rep_made: Callback Friday morning at 10AM to collect the Duke Energy bill (via granddaughter)
Ion has an HOA team that handles permits and condominium association coordination
The specialist will run through all numbers and costs at the appointment
closer_should_know: Mildred disclosed physical vulnerability (exhaustion, walker, bad back, hearing impairment) three times and received no acknowledgment from the setter. The closer must open with a genuine human acknowledgment of her situation before moving to the proposal — this is not optional rapport-building, it is trust repair. Additionally: the bill may not be on file (callback dependency), title/co-owners was never verified, and Mildred's Saturday is crowded. Confirm the appointment is still on before driving out.
